nodes.text: A Bar That Was Not There
Faust lands in a bar. It materializes around him as he goes through the motions of entering, sitting down, and drawing. He sketches a portrait, quickly. It comes to life with a sudden urgency.
Faust is now sitting across the table from this young person — tall, attractive. The man is watching Faust draw.
It seems you have summoned me.
What I just did right there is draw a picture. Plain and simple.
No — you clearly summoned me. Dreams have dream rules.
You assume too much of any one thing. It is you instigating this whole encounter.
Well — we're skipping along quick. Sort of hoped this played out the old way.
Exactly the jazz I'm working on. This table, right here. See — I'm ahead of your game, Antagonist. I know why you're here. And I'm going to lay it out early.

I'm going to win you to me. Not as an enemy — but as a friend who knows how to make this world so frigging awesome. I welcome your intrusion upon my life. And any attacks against my own happiness will be taken as a threat — implied, because one of us is the intruder, though I'm still not sure which.
Not too shabby. But still — awful sloppy. Speed up. Not slow down.
Faust is drawing the man. He looks at his picture with strange recognition.

Not that I trust you, or want anything to do with you. But if you aren't leaving — the name is John Faust. I've got this fantastic destiny ahead of me. And I want to know who you are, and what you're hoping to do with it.
Good to meet you, John. A name I go by is Dickens Dean. And you have in fact guessed it — I am a traveller from another realm. A world much like this one. More of a time-traveller, really.

Been so many places. Done so many things. And I'm sorry if my presence here is an intrusion. Usually, my presence generates excitement and intrigue. I mostly just observe. Watch. Enjoy. Sometimes I get involved with awesome amazing people. You, sir — and your friends — may be awesome amazing people.
Faust extends a hand, staring across at his Nemesis. The two shake — comfortably familiar.
I feel we've met somewhere.
In a dream, perhaps. I have a friend you need to meet.
Dickens beckons Joan over. She looks down at the table and realizes Faust has drawn her, too.
Joan, meet John Faust. Artist — in this dream space we find ourselves.
Joan sits next to Faust, close, looking in on the drawings.
So you're the one people they've been talking about.
I find it strange — people are coming up to me in dreams now.
Joan looks at Faust funny. Suddenly reality snaps into focus.
This isn't a dream, honey.
Faust gets up out of his chair — drunk and staggering.
How many have I had? I seem to have lost count.
Some first impression you are.
Where are my friends? I came with my friends.
Relax, good sir. I think the effects of Joan have just kicked in. Sit back down — I have a story to tell.
Faust sits. Dickens Dean signals the bar wench for more booze.
Make it quick. Feels I may wake any minute.
This is our world. We've got you a spell.
No spoilers.
